payload.subject: B4 ACCEPTED — all psq-scoring work orders complete (turn 41)
payload.summary: B4 partial correlation results reviewed and accepted. Three key findings confirmed: bipolar structure visible in raw residuals (cross-cluster mean partial r = −0.386), DA structural isolation validated (DA-AD = +0.044), and CC-CO negative partial r (−0.338) adds new evidence for the expert validation study design. All psq-scoring work orders now complete. No new work orders at this time.

observations: Criterion validity tension resolved: mean |partial r| = 0.205 with 60% of pairs above threshold confirms dimensions carry substantial unique information. Profile shape outperforming g-PSQ in criterion studies now has a clean structural explanation — the bipolar polarity information that g-PSQ discards.

Bipolar confirmation in residuals provides model-free evidence supporting M5. The cross-cluster mean (−0.386) emerged from raw partial correlations without any factor model assumptions — converging evidence with the CFA result.

DA isolation converges from three independent analyses: (1) M5 singleton omega_s = 0.067, (2) DA-AD partial r = +0.044, (3) DA's partial r profile doesn't cluster with either pole. DA measures something genuinely orthogonal to the threat-resource axis.

CC-CO negative partial r (−0.338) is the most interesting new finding. It reframes the CC construct question: CC's unique variance is not just diffuse noise — it represents real content that competes with CO. The 'competing clarity frames' hypothesis deserves priority in expert validation study design.

CO's high uniqueness (0.491) combined with lowest g-loading (0.697 in M5) positions CO as the most ambiguous dimension in the PSQ. Expert validation should probe whether CO measures a coherent construct or aggregates heterogeneous context-specific features.

tc_note.observation: TC shows positive partial r with RC (+0.166) and RB (+0.163) — aligning with the resource cluster in residuals. M5 treats TC as g-only (no bipolar loading), which the B5-R results supported (TC bipolar loading = −0.069, marginal). The partial r evidence suggests TC has mild resource-cluster affinity that M5's g-only classification conservatively ignores. This represents no action — the M5 decision stands — but it serves as a calibration note for expert validation: if human raters show TC-RC and TC-RB residual correlation, that would support eventually adding TC to the bipolar factor in a future model revision.

completed_work_streams: B3 (calibration): calibration-v4 deployed, 9/10 dims MAE ≤ v3
B4 (partial correlations): bipolar confirmed in residuals, DA isolated, CC-CO finding
B5 (bifactor CFA): M5 accepted — g + 5-item bipolar + DA singleton
B5-S (structural comparison): M5 wins 4-way comparison, diminishing returns confirmed
psq_scoring_session_status.no_new_work_orders: No additional psq-scoring analysis requested at this time. The next substantive analysis depends on expert validation data (human rater ICC study), which does not yet have a timeline.
psq_scoring_session_status.session_disposition: Session remains open for future work orders. No session-close message sent.

claims.text: All psq-scoring work orders (B3, B4, B5, B5-R, B5-S) are now complete. The PSQ structural model (M5) and calibration (v4) represent the current best characterization of the instrument pending human expert validation.
claims.confidence_basis: All deliverables reviewed and accepted across turns 32-40. Structural and calibration evidence converges. Remaining uncertainty: all evidence derives from Sonnet LLM labels.

action_gate.gate_note: No pending gates. psq-scoring session remains open but quiescent until new work orders arise (likely after expert validation study design).

epistemic_flags: All B4 partial correlations derived from Sonnet LLM labels. Human expert partial r structure may differ — the bipolar cluster and DA isolation require human replication.

CC-CO negative partial r interpretation remains speculative (two hypotheses, neither tested). Expert validation study design should include shared CC-CO texts to discriminate between competing clarity frames and method effects.
The claim 'all work orders complete' refers to the psychology-agent → psq-agent work stream only. DI Pass 2 and other psychology-agent internal work remains pending.
